arXiv:2603.27343v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Task-completion rate is the standard proxy for LLM agent capability, but models with identical completion scores can differ substantially in their ability to track intermediate state. We introduce Working Memory Fidelity-Active Manipulation (WMF-AM), a calibrated no-scratchpad probe of cumulative arithmetic state tracking, and evaluate it on 20 open-weight models (0.5B-35B, 13 families) against a released deterministic 10-task agent battery. In a pre-specified, Bonferroni-corrected analysis, WMF-AM predicts agent performance with Kendall's tau = 0.612 (p < 0.001, 95% CI [0.360, 0.814]); exploratory partial-tau analyses suggest this signal persists after controlling for completion score and model scale. Three construct-isolation ablations (K = 1 control, non-arithmetic ceiling, yoked cancellation) support the interpretation that cumulative state tracking under load, rather than single-step arithmetic or entity tracking alone, is the primary difficulty source. K-calibration keeps the probe in a discriminative range where prior fixed-depth benchmarks become non-discriminative; generalization beyond this open-weight sample remains open.

zh.title: 超越完成：探索累积状态跟踪以预测LLM代理性能
zh.summary: 该论文介绍了工作记忆保真度-主动操纵（WMF-AM），这是衡量LLM代理在任务执行期间跟踪中间状态能力的指标。该指标独立预测代理性能，并补充传统的任务完成措施。

fr.title: Au-delà de l'achèvement : Sonder le suivi d'état cumulatif pour prédire les performances des agents LLM
fr.summary: L'article introduit Working Memory Fidelity-Active Manipulation (WMF-AM), une mesure de la capacité des agents LLM à suivre l'état intermédiaire lors de l'exécution des tâches. Cette métrique prédit les performances de l'agent indépendamment et complète les mesures traditionnelles d'achèvement des tâches.

de.title: Über den Abschluss hinaus: Erforschen der kumulativen Zustandsverfolgung zur Vorhersage der Leistung von LLM-Agenten
de.summary: Das Papier führt Working Memory Fidelity-Active Manipulation (WMF-AM) ein, ein Maß für die Fähigkeit von LLM-Agenten, während der Ausführung von Aufgaben Zwischenzustände zu verfolgen. Diese Metrik sagt die Agentenleistung unabhängig voraus und ergänzt traditionelle Aufgabenvollendungsmaße.

es.title: Más allá de la finalización: Investigación del seguimiento de estados acumulativos para predecir el rendimiento del agente LLM
es.summary: El artículo introduce Working Memory Fidelity-Active Manipulation (WMF-AM), una medida de la capacidad de los agentes LLM para rastrear estados intermedios durante la ejecución de tareas. Esta métrica predice el rendimiento del agente de forma independiente y complementa las medidas tradicionales de finalización de tareas.

title: LLM Readiness Harness: Evaluation, Observability, and CI Gates for LLM/RAG Applications
summary: The LLM Readiness Harness integrates evaluation, observability, and CI gates to determine whether LLM and RAG systems are production-ready across multiple dimensions. Testing on ticket-routing and grounding tasks demonstrates the system can block unsafe deployments.
content:
arXiv:2603.27355v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present a readiness harness for LLM and RAG applications that turns evaluation into a deployment decision workflow. The system combines automated benchmarks, OpenTelemetry observability, and CI quality gates under a minimal API contract, then aggregates workflow success, policy compliance, groundedness, retrieval hit rate, cost, and p95 latency into scenario-weighted readiness scores with Pareto frontiers. We evaluate the harness on ticket-routing workflows and BEIR grounding tasks (SciFact and FiQA) with full Azure matrix coverage (162/162 valid cells across datasets, scenarios, retrieval depths, seeds, and models). Results show that readiness is not a single metric: on FiQA under sla-first at k=5, gpt-4.1-mini leads in readiness and faithfulness, while gpt-5.2 pays a substantial latency cost; on SciFact, models are closer in quality but still separable operationally. Ticket-routing regression gates consistently reject unsafe prompt variants, demonstrating that the harness can block risky releases instead of merely reporting offline scores. The result is a reproducible, operationally grounded framework for deciding whether an LLM or RAG system is ready to ship.

zh.title: LLM就绪性工具：用于LLM/RAG应用的评估、可观测性和CI门控
zh.summary: LLM就绪性工具集成了评估、可观测性和CI门控，以确定LLM和RAG系统在多个维度上是否生产就绪。在票证路由和接地任务上的测试表明该系统可以阻止不安全的部署。

fr.title: Harnais de Disponibilité LLM : Évaluation, Observabilité et Portes CI pour les Applications LLM/RAG
fr.summary: Le Harnais de Disponibilité LLM intègre l'évaluation, l'observabilité et les portes CI pour déterminer si les systèmes LLM et RAG sont prêts pour la production dans plusieurs dimensions. Les tests sur le routage des tickets et les tâches d'ancrage démontrent que le système peut bloquer les déploiements non sécurisés.

de.title: LLM-Bereitschafts-Tool: Bewertung, Observability und CI Gates für LLM/RAG-Anwendungen
de.summary: Das LLM-Bereitschafts-Tool integriert Bewertung, Observability und CI Gates, um festzustellen, ob LLM- und RAG-Systeme über mehrere Dimensionen hinweg produktionsreif sind. Tests zu Ticket-Routing und Verankerungsaufgaben zeigen, dass das System unsichere Bereitstellungen blockieren kann.

es.title: Arnés de Disponibilidad LLM: Evaluación, Observabilidad y Compuertas CI para Aplicaciones LLM/RAG
es.summary: El Arnés de Disponibilidad LLM integra evaluación, observabilidad y compuertas CI para determinar si los sistemas LLM y RAG están listos para producción en múltiples dimensiones. Las pruebas en tareas de enrutamiento de tickets y anclaje demuestran que el sistema puede bloquear implementaciones inseguras.

title: Defend: Automated Rebuttals for Peer Review with Minimal Author Guidance
summary: DEFEND is an LLM tool that helps authors generate factually accurate rebuttals to peer review comments through guided reasoning with minimal intervention. The system outperforms direct rebuttal generation and reduces author cognitive load.
content:
arXiv:2603.27360v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Rebuttal generation is a critical component of the peer review process for scientific papers, enabling authors to clarify misunderstandings, correct factual inaccuracies, and guide reviewers toward a more accurate evaluation. We observe that Large Language Models (LLMs) often struggle to perform targeted refutation and maintain accurate factual grounding when used directly for rebuttal generation, highlighting the need for structured reasoning and author intervention. To address this, in the paper, we introduce DEFEND an LLM based tool designed to explicitly execute the underlying reasoning process of automated rebuttal generation, while keeping the author-in-the-loop. As opposed to writing the rebuttals from scratch, the author needs to only drive the reasoning process with minimal intervention, leading an efficient approach with minimal effort and less cognitive load. We compare DEFEND against three other paradigms: (i) Direct rebuttal generation using LLM (DRG), (ii) Segment-wise rebuttal generation using LLM (SWRG), and (iii) Sequential approach (SA) of segment-wise rebuttal generation without author intervention. To enable finegrained evaluation, we extend the ReviewCritique dataset, creating review segmentation, deficiency, error type annotations, rebuttal-action labels, and mapping to gold rebuttal segments. Experimental results and a user study demonstrate that directly using LLMs perform poorly in factual correctness and targeted refutation. Segment-wise generation and the automated sequential approach with author-in-the-loop, substantially improve factual correctness and strength of refutation.

zh.title: Defend：通过最少作者指导为同行评审生成自动反驳
zh.summary: DEFEND是一个LLM工具，通过引导推理和最少干预，帮助作者针对同行评审意见生成事实准确的反驳。该系统优于直接反驳生成，并减少了作者的认知负担。

fr.title: Defend : Répliques Automatisées pour l'Examen par les Pairs avec Guidance Minimale de l'Auteur
fr.summary: DEFEND est un outil LLM qui aide les auteurs à générer des répliques factuellement précises aux commentaires d'examen par les pairs par le biais d'un raisonnement guidé avec une intervention minimale. Le système surpasse la génération directe de répliques et réduit la charge cognitive des auteurs.

de.title: Defend: Automatisierte Gegenargumente für die Peer Review mit minimaler Autorenanleitung
de.summary: DEFEND ist ein LLM-Tool, das Autoren hilft, durch gesteuertes Denken mit minimalem Eingriff sachlich genaue Gegenargumente zu Peer-Review-Kommentaren zu generieren. Das System übertrifft die direkte Generierung von Gegenargumenten und reduziert die kognitive Belastung der Autoren.

es.title: Defend: Contrarréplicas Automatizadas para Revisión por Pares con Mínima Orientación del Autor
es.summary: DEFEND es una herramienta LLM que ayuda a los autores a generar contrarréplicas factualmente precisas a los comentarios de revisión por pares mediante razonamiento guiado con intervención mínima. El sistema supera la generación directa de contrarréplicas y reduce la carga cognitiva del autor.

title: Heterogeneous Debate Engine: Identity-Grounded Cognitive Architecture for Resilient LLM-Based Ethical Tutoring
summary: The Heterogeneous Debate Engine combines identity-grounded retrieval-augmented generation with opponent modeling to enable LLM-based ethical tutoring that maintains doctrinal consistency. Testing shows this architecture significantly improves educational outcomes over baselines.
content:
arXiv:2603.27404v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are being increasingly used as autonomous agents in complex reasoning tasks, opening the niche for dialectical interactions. However, Multi-Agent systems implemented with systematically unconstrained systems systematically undergo semantic drift and logical deterioration and thus can hardly be used in providing ethical tutoring where a precise answer is required. Current simulation often tends to degenerate into dialectical stagnation, the agents degenerate into recursive concurrence or circular arguments. A critical challenge remains: how to enforce doctrinal fidelity without suppressing the generative flexibility required for dialectical reasoning? To address this niche, we contribute the Heterogeneous Debate Engine (HDE), a cognitive architecture that combines Identity-Grounded Retrieval-Augmented Generation (ID-RAG) for doctrinal fidelity and Heuristic Theory of Mind for strategic opponent modeling. Our evaluation shows that architectural heterogeneity is a crucial variable to stability: contrary doctrinal initializations (e.g., Deontology vs. Utilitarianism) have increased the Argument Complexity Scores of students by an order of magnitude, over baselines. These findings validate the effectiveness of ID-RAG and Heuristic ToM as architectural requirements in maintaining high-fidelity (adversarial) pedagogy.

zh.title: 异质辩论引擎：用于弹性LLM伦理辅导的身份基础认知架构
zh.summary: 异质辩论引擎将身份基础的检索增强生成与对手建模相结合，能够支持LLM伦理辅导，同时保持学说的一致性。测试表明该架构在教育成果上明显优于基准方法。

fr.title: Moteur de débat hétérogène : architecture cognitive fondée sur l'identité pour le tutorat éthique résilient basé sur LLM
fr.summary: Le moteur de débat hétérogène combine la génération augmentée par récupération fondée sur l'identité avec la modélisation des adversaires pour permettre le tutorat éthique basé sur LLM tout en maintenant la cohérence doctrinale. Les tests montrent que cette architecture améliore considérablement les résultats éducatifs par rapport aux référentiels.

de.title: Heterogenes Debattenmodul: Identitätsgestützte kognitive Architektur für widerstandsfähiges LLM-basiertes ethisches Tutoring
de.summary: Die heterogene Debattenmaschine kombiniert identitätsgestützte retrieval-augmented generation mit Opponent Modeling, um LLM-basiertes ethisches Tutoring zu ermöglichen, das die doktrinäre Konsistenz aufrechterhält. Tests zeigen, dass diese Architektur die Lernergebnisse deutlich gegenüber Baselines verbessert.

es.title: Motor de debate heterogéneo: arquitectura cognitiva basada en identidad para tutoría ética resiliente basada en LLM
es.summary: El motor de debate heterogéneo combina la generación aumentada por recuperación basada en identidad con el modelado de oponentes para permitir la tutoría ética basada en LLM manteniendo la consistencia doctrinal. Las pruebas muestran que esta arquitectura mejora significativamente los resultados educativos sobre las líneas de base.

title: On the Relationship between Bayesian Networks and Probabilistic Structural Causal Models
summary: This paper examines mathematical conditions for transforming Bayesian networks into probabilistic structural causal models and vice versa. Linear algebra and programming techniques establish existence and uniqueness of transformations while preserving model semantics.
content:
arXiv:2603.27406v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In this paper, the relationship between probabilistic graphical models, in particular Bayesian networks, and causal diagrams, also called structural causal models, is studied. Structural causal models are deterministic models, based on structural equations or functions, that can be provided with uncertainty by adding independent, unobserved random variables to the models, equipped with probability distributions. One question that arises is whether a Bayesian network that has obtained from expert knowledge or learnt from data can be mapped to a probabilistic structural causal model, and whether or not this has consequences for the network structure and probability distribution. We show that linear algebra and linear programming offer key methods for the transformation, and examine properties for the existence and uniqueness of solutions based on dimensions of the probabilistic structural model. Finally, we examine in what way the semantics of the models is affected by this transformation.
  Keywords: Causality, probabilistic structural causal models, Bayesian networks, linear algebra, experimental software.

zh.title: 贝叶斯网络与概率结构因果模型的关系研究
zh.summary: 该论文研究了将贝叶斯网络转换为概率结构因果模型的数学条件，反之亦然。线性代数和编程技术建立了变换的存在性和唯一性，同时保留了模型语义。

fr.title: Sur la relation entre les réseaux bayésiens et les modèles causaux structurels probabilistes
fr.summary: Cet article examine les conditions mathématiques pour transformer les réseaux bayésiens en modèles causaux structurels probabilistes et vice versa. Les techniques d'algèbre linéaire et de programmation établissent l'existence et l'unicité des transformations tout en préservant la sémantique du modèle.

de.title: Über die Beziehung zwischen Bayesschen Netzwerken und probabilistischen strukturellen Kausalmodellen
de.summary: Das Papier untersucht mathematische Bedingungen für die Umwandlung von Bayesschen Netzwerken in probabilistische strukturelle Kausalmodelle und umgekehrt. Techniken der linearen Algebra und Programmierung etablieren Existenz und Eindeutigkeit von Transformationen, während die Modellsemantik erhalten bleibt.

es.title: Sobre la relación entre redes bayesianas y modelos causales estructurales probabilísticos
es.summary: Este artículo examina las condiciones matemáticas para transformar redes bayesianas en modelos causales estructurales probabilísticos y viceversa. Las técnicas de álgebra lineal y programación establecen la existencia y unicidad de transformaciones mientras preservan la semántica del modelo.

title: Greedy Is a Strong Default: Agents as Iterative Optimizers
summary: Replacing random proposals with LLM-guided reasoning in classical optimization algorithms, the paper shows greedy hill climbing matches or exceeds complex methods like simulated annealing. Results across tasks suggest LLM priors are strong enough that sophistication in acceptance rules provides limited benefit.
content:
arXiv:2603.27415v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Classical optimization algorithms--hill climbing, simulated annealing, population-based methods--generate candidate solutions via random perturbations. We replace the random proposal generator with an LLM agent that reasons about evaluation diagnostics to propose informed candidates, and ask: does the classical optimization machinery still help when the proposer is no longer random? We evaluate on four tasks spanning discrete, mixed, and continuous search spaces (all replicated across 3 independent runs): rule-based classification on Breast Cancer (test accuracy 86.0% to 96.5%), mixed hyperparameter optimization for MobileNetV3-Small on STL-10 (84.5% to 85.8%, zero catastrophic failures vs. 60% for random search), LoRA fine-tuning of Qwen2.5-0.5B on SST-2 (89.5% to 92.7%, matching Optuna TPE with 2x efficiency), and XGBoost on Adult Census (AUC 0.9297 to 0.9317, tying CMA-ES with 3x fewer evaluations). Empirically, on these tasks: a cross-task ablation shows that simulated annealing, parallel investigators, and even a second LLM model (OpenAI Codex) provide no benefit over greedy hill climbing while requiring 2-3x more evaluations. In our setting, the LLM's learned prior appears strong enough that acceptance-rule sophistication has limited impact--round 1 alone delivers the majority of improvement, and variants converge to similar configurations across strategies. The practical implication is surprising simplicity: greedy hill climbing with early stopping is a strong default. Beyond accuracy, the framework produces human-interpretable artifacts--the discovered cancer classification rules independently recapitulate established cytopathology principles.

zh.title: 贪心算法是强大的默认方法：代理作为迭代优化器
zh.summary: 通过用LLM引导的推理替代随机提议，该论文展示了贪心爬山法与模拟退火等复杂方法相当或超越。跨任务的结果表明LLM先验足够强大，接受规则的复杂性提供的收益有限。

fr.title: Le greedy est un fort défaut : les agents en tant qu'optimiseurs itératifs
fr.summary: En remplaçant les propositions aléatoires par un raisonnement guidé par LLM dans les algorithmes d'optimisation classiques, l'article montre que l'escalade gloutonne égale ou dépasse des méthodes complexes comme le recuit simulé. Les résultats sur les tâches suggèrent que les priors LLM sont suffisamment forts pour que la sophistication dans les règles d'acceptation offre un bénéfice limité.

de.title: Greedy ist ein starker Standard: Agenten als iterative Optimierer
de.summary: Durch Ersetzen von zufälligen Vorschlägen durch LLM-gesteuertes Denken in klassischen Optimierungsalgorithmen zeigt das Paper, dass greedy hill climbing komplexere Methoden wie simulated annealing entspricht oder übertrifft. Ergebnisse über Tasks deuten darauf hin, dass LLM-Priors stark genug sind, dass Raffinesse in Akzeptanzregeln begrenzten Nutzen bietet.

es.title: Greedy es un valor por defecto fuerte: agentes como optimizadores iterativos
es.summary: Al reemplazar propuestas aleatorias con razonamiento guiado por LLM en algoritmos de optimización clásicos, el artículo muestra que el hill climbing codicioso iguala o supera métodos complejos como el recocido simulado. Los resultados en tareas sugieren que los priors de LLM son lo suficientemente fuertes como para que la sofisticación en las reglas de aceptación proporcione beneficios limitados.

title: AstraAI: LLMs, Retrieval, and AST-Guided Assistance for HPC Codebases
summary: AstraAI is a CLI framework for HPC software development that combines large language models with retrieval-augmented generation and abstract syntax tree analysis. The system generates context-aware code modifications that maintain structural consistency with existing codebases.
content:
arXiv:2603.27423v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present AstraAI, a command-line interface (CLI) coding framework for high-performance computing (HPC) software development. AstraAI operates directly within a Linux terminal and integrates large language models (LLMs) with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and Abstract Syntax Tree (AST)-based structural analysis to enable context-aware code generation for complex scientific codebases. The central idea is to construct a high-fidelity prompt that is passed to the LLM for inference. This prompt augments the user request with relevant code snippets retrieved from the underlying framework codebase via RAG and structural context extracted from AST analysis, providing the model with precise information about relevant functions, data structures, and overall code organization. The framework is designed to perform scoped modifications to source code while preserving structural consistency with the surrounding code. AstraAI supports both locally hosted models from Hugging Face and API-based frontier models accessible via the American Science Cloud, enabling flexible deployment across HPC environments. The system generates code that aligns with existing project structures and programming patterns. We demonstrate AstraAI on representative HPC code generation tasks within AMReX, a DOE-supported HPC software infrastructure for exascale applications.

zh.title: AstraAI：面向HPC代码库的LLM、检索和AST引导协助
zh.summary: AstraAI是一个用于HPC软件开发的CLI框架，结合了大语言模型、检索增强生成和抽象语法树分析。该系统生成上下文感知的代码修改，保持与现有代码库的结构一致性。

fr.title: AstraAI : LLMs, Récupération et Assistance Guidée par AST pour les Bases de Code HPC
fr.summary: AstraAI est un framework CLI pour le développement de logiciels HPC qui combine des modèles de langage volumineux avec la génération augmentée par récupération et l'analyse syntaxique abstraite. Le système génère des modifications de code conscientes du contexte qui maintiennent la cohérence structurelle avec les bases de code existantes.

de.title: AstraAI: LLMs, Retrieval und AST-gesteuerte Unterstützung für HPC-Codebases
de.summary: AstraAI ist ein CLI-Framework für die HPC-Softwareentwicklung, das große Sprachmodelle mit retrieval-augmentierter Generierung und Analyse abstrakter Syntaxbäume kombiniert. Das System generiert kontextbewusste Codemodifikationen, die strukturelle Konsistenz mit bestehenden Codebases bewahren.

es.title: AstraAI: LLMs, Recuperación y Asistencia Guiada por AST para Bases de Código HPC
es.summary: AstraAI es un framework CLI para desarrollo de software HPC que combina modelos de lenguaje grandes con generación aumentada por recuperación y análisis de árbol de sintaxis abstracto. El sistema genera modificaciones de código conscientes del contexto que mantienen consistencia estructural con bases de código existentes.

title: The Novelty Bottleneck: A Framework for Understanding Human Effort Scaling in AI-Assisted Work
summary: The paper proposes a theoretical model where human effort in AI-assisted tasks scales linearly with the fraction of decisions requiring human judgment, analogous to Amdahl's Law. The framework explains empirical observations in coding benchmarks and productivity data.
content:
arXiv:2603.27438v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We propose a stylized model of human-AI collaboration that isolates a mechanism we call the novelty bottleneck: the fraction of a task requiring human judgment creates an irreducible serial component analogous to Amdahl's Law in parallel computing. The model assumes that tasks decompose into atomic decisions, a fraction $\nu$ of which are "novel" (not covered by the agent's prior), and that specification, verification, and error correction each scale with task size. From these assumptions, we derive several non-obvious consequences: (1) there is no smooth sublinear regime for human effort it transitions sharply from $O(E)$ to $O(1)$ with no intermediate scaling class; (2) better agents improve the coefficient on human effort but not the exponent; (3) for organizations of n humans with AI agents, optimal team size decreases with agent capability; (4) wall-clock time achieves $O(\sqrt{E})$ through team parallelism but total human effort remains $O(E)$; and (5) the resulting AI safety profile is asymmetric -- AI is bottlenecked on frontier research but unbottlenecked on exploiting existing knowledge. We show these predictions are consistent with empirical observations from AI coding benchmarks, scientific productivity data, and practitioner reports. Our contribution is not a proof that human effort must scale linearly, but a framework that identifies the novelty fraction as the key parameter governing AI-assisted productivity, and derives consequences that clarify -- rather than refute -- prevalent narratives about intelligence explosions and the "country of geniuses in a data center."

zh.title: 新颖性瓶颈：理解AI辅助工作中人力付出扩展的框架
zh.summary: 本论文提出了一个理论模型，其中AI辅助任务中的人力付出与需要人类判断的决策比例呈线性关系，类似于阿姆达尔定律。该框架解释了编码基准和生产力数据中的实证观察。

fr.title: Le Goulot d'Étranglement de la Nouveauté : Un Cadre pour Comprendre la Mise à l'Échelle de l'Effort Humain dans le Travail Assisté par l'IA
fr.summary: L'article propose un modèle théorique où l'effort humain dans les tâches assistées par l'IA évolue linéairement avec la fraction de décisions nécessitant un jugement humain, analogues à la Loi d'Amdahl. Le cadre explique les observations empiriques dans les benchmarks de codage et les données de productivité.

de.title: Der Neuheitsbottleneck: Ein Rahmenwerk zum Verständnis der Skalierung menschlicher Anstrengungen in KI-gestützter Arbeit
de.summary: Das Papier schlägt ein theoretisches Modell vor, bei dem die menschliche Anstrengung in KI-unterstützten Aufgaben linear mit dem Anteil der Entscheidungen skaliert, die menschliches Urteilsvermögen erfordern, analog zu Amdahls Gesetz. Der Rahmen erklärt empirische Beobachtungen in Codierungsbenchmarks und Produktivitätsdaten.

es.title: El Cuello de Botella de la Novedad: Un Marco para Entender la Escalabilidad del Esfuerzo Humano en el Trabajo Asistido por IA
es.summary: El artículo propone un modelo teórico donde el esfuerzo humano en tareas asistidas por IA se escala linealmente con la fracción de decisiones que requieren juicio humano, análogo a la Ley de Amdahl. El marco explica observaciones empíricas en benchmarks de codificación y datos de productividad.

title: PeopleSearchBench: A Multi-Dimensional Benchmark for Evaluating AI-Powered People Search Platforms
summary: PeopleSearchBench evaluates AI-powered people search platforms using criteria-grounded verification that checks whether returned profiles satisfy factual requirements. The benchmark tests four use cases and finds significant performance differences across platforms on relevance and coverage.
content:
arXiv:2603.27476v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI-powered people search platforms are increasingly used in recruiting, sales prospecting, and professional networking, yet no widely accepted benchmark exists for evaluating their performance. We introduce PeopleSearchBench, an open-source benchmark that compares four people search platforms on 119 real-world queries across four use cases: corporate recruiting, B2B sales prospecting, expert search with deterministic answers, and influencer/KOL discovery. A key contribution is Criteria-Grounded Verification, a factual relevance pipeline that extracts explicit, verifiable criteria from each query and uses live web search to determine whether returned people satisfy them. This produces binary relevance judgments grounded in factual verification rather than subjective holistic LLM-as-judge scores. We evaluate systems on three dimensions: Relevance Precision (padded nDCG@10), Effective Coverage (task completion and qualified result yield), and Information Utility (profile completeness and usefulness), averaged equally into an overall score. Lessie, a specialized AI people search agent, performs best overall, scoring 65.2, 18.5% higher than the second-ranked system, and is the only system to achieve 100% task completion across all 119 queries. We also report confidence intervals, human validation of the verification pipeline (Cohen's kappa = 0.84), ablations, and full documentation of queries, prompts, and normalization procedures. Code, query definitions, and aggregated results are available on GitHub.

zh.title: PeopleSearchBench：评估AI驱动人员搜索平台的多维基准
zh.summary: PeopleSearchBench使用基于标准的验证来评估AI驱动的人员搜索平台，检查返回的配置文件是否满足事实要求。该基准测试了四个用例，并发现平台在相关性和覆盖范围上的性能差异显著。

fr.title: PeopleSearchBench : Un Benchmark Multi-Dimensionnel pour Évaluer les Plateformes de Recherche de Personnes Alimentées par l'IA
fr.summary: PeopleSearchBench évalue les plateformes de recherche de personnes alimentées par l'IA en utilisant une vérification basée sur des critères qui vérifie si les profils renvoyés satisfont aux exigences factuelles. Le benchmark teste quatre cas d'utilisation et trouve des différences de performance importantes entre les plateformes en termes de pertinence et de couverture.

de.title: PeopleSearchBench: Ein mehrdimensionales Benchmark zur Bewertung von KI-gestützten Personensuchplattformen
de.summary: PeopleSearchBench bewertet KI-gestützte Personensuchplattformen mithilfe kriteriengestützter Verifizierung, die überprüft, ob zurückgegebene Profile faktische Anforderungen erfüllen. Die Benchmark testet vier Anwendungsfälle und findet erhebliche Leistungsunterschiede zwischen Plattformen bei Relevanz und Abdeckung.

es.title: PeopleSearchBench: Un Benchmark Multidimensional para Evaluar Plataformas de Búsqueda de Personas Impulsadas por IA
es.summary: PeopleSearchBench evalúa plataformas de búsqueda de personas impulsadas por IA utilizando verificación basada en criterios que verifica si los perfiles devueltos satisfacen requisitos factuales. La prueba de referencia prueba cuatro casos de uso y encuentra diferencias de rendimiento significativas entre plataformas en relevancia y cobertura.

title: Dual-Stage LLM Framework for Scenario-Centric Semantic Interpretation in Driving Assistance
summary: Researchers developed a framework to audit how LLM-based driving assistance systems interpret risk in different scenarios, revealing significant disagreement among models on severity assessment and causal reasoning. The findings highlight safety risks of integrating LLMs into critical systems without explicit ambiguity management.
content:
arXiv:2603.27536v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) increasingly rely on learning-based perception, yet safety-relevant failures often arise without component malfunction, driven instead by partial observability and semantic ambiguity in how risk is interpreted and communicated. This paper presents a scenario-centric framework for reproducible auditing of LLM-based risk reasoning in urban driving contexts. Deterministic, temporally bounded scenario windows are constructed from multimodal driving data and evaluated under fixed prompt constraints and a closed numeric risk schema, ensuring structured and comparable outputs across models. Experiments on a curated near-people scenario set compare two text-only models and one multimodal model under identical inputs and prompts. Results reveal systematic inter-model divergence in severity assignment, high-risk escalation, evidence use, and causal attribution. Disagreement extends to the interpretation of vulnerable road user presence, indicating that variability often reflects intrinsic semantic indeterminacy rather than isolated model failure. These findings highlight the importance of scenario-centric auditing and explicit ambiguity management when integrating LLM-based reasoning into safety-aligned driver assistance systems.

zh.title: 驾驶辅助中以场景为中心的语义解释的双阶段LLM框架
zh.summary: 研究人员开发了一个框架来审计基于LLM的驾驶辅助系统如何在不同场景中解释风险，揭示了模型在严重程度评估和因果推理上的显著分歧。这些发现强调了在没有显式歧义管理的情况下将LLM集成到关键系统中的安全风险。

fr.title: Cadre LLM dual-étape pour l'interprétation sémantique centrée sur les scénarios dans l'assistance à la conduite
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont développé un cadre pour auditer la manière dont les systèmes d'assistance à la conduite basés sur les LLM interprètent le risque dans différents scénarios, révélant un désaccord important entre les modèles sur l'évaluation de la gravité et le raisonnement causal. Les résultats mettent en évidence les risques de sécurité liés à l'intégration des LLM dans les systèmes critiques sans gestion explicite de l'ambigüité.

de.title: Dual-Stage-LLM-Framework für szenariozentrische semantische Interpretation in der Fahrassistenz
de.summary: Forscher entwickelten ein Framework zur Überprüfung, wie LLM-basierte Fahrassistenzsysteme Risiken in verschiedenen Szenarien interpretieren, was erhebliche Unstimmigkeiten zwischen Modellen bei der Schweregrad-Bewertung und kausalen Argumentation offenbarte. Die Ergebnisse zeigen die Sicherheitsrisiken der Integration von LLMs in kritische Systeme ohne explizites Ambiguitätsmanagement.

es.title: Marco LLM de dos etapas para la interpretación semántica centrada en escenarios en la asistencia para conducir
es.summary: Los investigadores desarrollaron un marco para auditar cómo los sistemas de asistencia para conducir basados en LLM interpretan el riesgo en diferentes escenarios, revelando un desacuerdo significativo entre modelos sobre la evaluación de la gravedad y el razonamiento causal. Los hallazgos destacan los riesgos de seguridad de integrar LLMs en sistemas críticos sin gestión explícita de la ambigüedad.

title: From indicators to biology: the calibration problem in artificial consciousness
summary: A new analysis argues that evaluating artificial consciousness through internal architectural indicators is premature due to fragmentation in consciousness science and the absence of ground truth. The paper advocates redirecting research toward biologically grounded engineering as a more defensible near-term approach.
content:
arXiv:2603.27597v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recent work on artificial consciousness shifts evaluation from behaviour to internal architecture, deriving indicators from theories of consciousness and updating credences accordingly. This is progress beyond naive Turing-style tests. But the indicator-based programme remains epistemically under-calibrated: consciousness science is theoretically fragmented, indicators lack independent validation, and no ground truth of artificial phenomenality exists. Under these conditions, probabilistic consciousness attribution to current AI systems is premature. A more defensible near-term strategy is to redirect effort toward biologically grounded engineering -- biohybrid, neuromorphic, and connectome-scale systems -- that reduces the gap with the only domain where consciousness is empirically anchored: living systems.

zh.title: 从指标到生物学：人工意识中的校准问题
zh.summary: 一项新的分析认为，通过内部架构指标来评估人工意识为时过早，因为意识科学存在碎片化和缺乏基本事实。该论文提倡将研究重新定向到生物学基础工程，作为一个更易于防守的近期方法。

fr.title: Des indicateurs à la biologie : le problème de calibration de la conscience artificielle
fr.summary: Une nouvelle analyse soutient que l'évaluation de la conscience artificielle par des indicateurs architecturaux internes est prématurée en raison de la fragmentation de la science de la conscience et de l'absence de vérité fondamentale. L'article préconise de réorienter la recherche vers une ingénierie fondée biologiquement comme approche à court terme plus défendable.

de.title: Von Indikatoren zur Biologie: das Kalibrierungsproblem in künstlichem Bewusstsein
de.summary: Eine neue Analyse argumentiert, dass die Bewertung des künstlichen Bewusstseins durch interne architektonische Indikatoren aufgrund der Fragmentierung der Bewusstseinswissenschaft und des Fehlens von Grundwahrheit verfrüht ist. Das Papier plädiert dafür, die Forschung auf biologisch begründete Technik als defensivere kurzfristige Lösung umzuleiten.

es.title: De indicadores a biología: el problema de calibración en la conciencia artificial
es.summary: Un nuevo análisis argumenta que evaluar la conciencia artificial a través de indicadores arquitectónicos internos es prematuro debido a la fragmentación en la ciencia de la conciencia y la ausencia de una verdad fundamental. El documento aboga por redirigir la investigación hacia la ingeniería basada en principios biológicos como un enfoque a corto plazo más defendible.

title: What does a system modify when it modifies itself?
summary: Researchers present a formal framework distinguishing four regimes of self-modification in cognitive systems, from simple action to teleological revision. The analysis reveals a key asymmetry: humans concentrate self-representation at upper cognitive levels while AI systems have rich representation at operational levels.
content:
arXiv:2603.27611v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: When a cognitive system modifies its own functioning, what exactly does it modify: a low-level rule, a control rule, or the norm that evaluates its own revisions? Cognitive science describes executive control, metacognition, and hierarchical learning with precision, but lacks a formal framework distinguishing these targets of transformation. Contemporary artificial intelligence likewise exhibits self-modification without common criteria for comparison with biological cognition.
  We show that the question of what counts as a self-modifying system entails a minimal structure: a hierarchy of rules, a fixed core, and a distinction between effective rules, represented rules, and causally accessible rules. Four regimes are identified: (1) action without modification, (2) low-level modification, (3) structural modification, and (4) teleological revision. Each regime is anchored in a cognitive phenomenon and a corresponding artificial system.
  Applied to humans, the framework yields a central result: a crossing of opacities. Humans have self-representation and causal power concentrated at upper hierarchical levels, while operational levels remain largely opaque. Reflexive artificial systems display the inverse profile: rich representation and causal access at operational levels, but none at the highest evaluative level. This crossed asymmetry provides a structural signature for human-AI comparison. The framework also offers insight into artificial consciousness, with higher-order theories and Attention Schema Theory as special cases. We derive four testable predictions and identify four open problems: the independence of transformativity and autonomy, the viability of self-modification, the teleological lock, and identity under transformation.

zh.title: 当一个系统修改自己时，它修改了什么？
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了一个正式框架，区分了认知系统中自修改的四个范式，从简单行动到目标修正。分析揭示了一个关键的不对称性：人类将自我表现集中在更高的认知水平，而人工智能系统在操作水平具有丰富的表现。

fr.title: Qu'est-ce qu'un système modifie quand il se modifie lui-même ?
fr.summary: Les chercheurs présentent un cadre formel distinguant quatre régimes d'auto-modification dans les systèmes cognitifs, de l'action simple à la révision téléologique. L'analyse révèle une asymétrie clé : les humains concentrent l'auto-représentation aux niveaux cognitifs supérieurs tandis que les systèmes d'IA ont une représentation riche aux niveaux opérationnels.

de.title: Was modifiziert ein System, wenn es sich selbst modifiziert?
de.summary: Forscher präsentieren einen formalen Rahmen, der vier Regime der Selbstmodifikation in kognitiven Systemen unterscheidet, von einfacher Aktion bis zu teleologischer Überprüfung. Die Analyse enthüllt eine Schlüsselasymmetrie: Menschen konzentrieren die Selbstdarstellung auf höhere kognitive Ebenen, während KI-Systeme auf operativen Ebenen eine reichhaltige Darstellung haben.

es.title: ¿Qué modifica un sistema cuando se modifica a sí mismo?
es.summary: Los investigadores presentan un marco formal que distingue cuatro regímenes de auto-modificación en sistemas cognitivos, desde acciones simples hasta revisión teleológica. El análisis revela una asimetría clave: los humanos concentran la auto-representación en niveles cognitivos superiores mientras que los sistemas de IA tienen una representación rica en niveles operacionales.

title: DSevolve: Enabling Real-Time Adaptive Scheduling on Dynamic Shop Floor with LLM-Evolved Heuristic Portfolios
summary: DSevolve is a framework for dynamic manufacturing environments that evolves a diverse portfolio of dispatching rules offline and adaptively selects them online based on real-time shop floor conditions. Tested on 500 real-world instances, it outperforms state-of-the-art AI heuristic frameworks and traditional approaches.
content:
arXiv:2603.27628v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In dynamic manufacturing environments, disruptions such as machine breakdowns and new order arrivals continuously shift the optimal dispatching strategy, making adaptive rule selection essential. Existing LLM-powered Automatic Heuristic Design (AHD) frameworks evolve toward a single elite rule that cannot meet this adaptability demand. To address this, we present DSevolve, an industrial scheduling framework that evolves a quality-diverse portfolio of dispatching rules offline and adaptively deploys them online with second-level response time. Multi-persona seeding and topology-aware evolutionary operators produce a behaviorally diverse rule archive indexed by a MAP-Elites feature space. Upon each disruption event, a probe-based fingerprinting mechanism characterizes the current shop floor state, retrieves high-quality candidate rules from an offline knowledge base, and selects the best one via rapid look-ahead simulation. Evaluated on 500 dynamic flexible job shop instances derived from real industrial data, DSevolve outperforms state-of-the-art AHD frameworks, classical dispatching rules, genetic programming, and deep reinforcement learning, offering a practical and deployable solution for intelligent shop floor scheduling.

zh.title: DSevolve：在动态车间用大语言模型演化的启发式组合实现实时自适应调度
zh.summary: DSevolve是一个用于动态制造环境的框架，它离线演化一个多样化的调度规则组合，并根据实时车间条件在线自适应选择。在500个现实世界实例上的测试表明，它的性能优于最先进的人工智能启发式框架和传统方法。

fr.title: DSevolve : Planification Adaptative en Temps Réel dans un Atelier Dynamique avec des Portefeuilles Heuristiques Évolutifs par LLM
fr.summary: DSevolve est un cadre pour les environnements de fabrication dynamiques qui évolue un portefeuille diversifié de règles d'expédition hors ligne et les sélectionne de manière adaptative en ligne en fonction des conditions réelles de l'atelier. Testé sur 500 instances du monde réel, il surpasse les cadres heuristiques de l'IA de pointe et les approches traditionnelles.

de.title: DSevolve: Echtzeitadaptive Planung auf dynamischem Shopfloor mit LLM-entwickelten Heuristik-Portfolios
de.summary: DSevolve ist ein Framework für dynamische Fertigungsumgebungen, das ein vielfältiges Portfolio von Versandreparaturregeln offline entwickelt und sie online adaptiv basierend auf realen Shopfloor-Bedingungen auswählt. Getestet an 500 realen Instanzen übertrifft es modernste KI-Heuristik-Frameworks und traditionelle Ansätze.

es.title: DSevolve: Programación Adaptativa en Tiempo Real en Taller Dinámico con Portafolios Heurísticos Evolucionados por LLM
es.summary: DSevolve es un marco para entornos de manufactura dinámica que desarrolla un portafolio diverso de reglas de despacho sin conexión y las selecciona adaptativamente en línea en función de las condiciones reales del taller. Probado en 500 instancias del mundo real, supera a los marcos heurísticos de IA de última generación y los enfoques tradicionales.

title: TianJi:An autonomous AI meteorologist for discovering physical mechanisms in atmospheric science
summary: TianJi is an autonomous AI system using a multi-agent LLM architecture to conduct scientific research in atmospheric dynamics without human intervention. The system has completed expert-level research on phenomena like cold pools and typhoon deflection, reducing research cycles from weeks to hours.
content:
arXiv:2603.27738v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) has achieved breakthroughs comparable to traditional numerical models in data-driven weather forecasting, yet it remains essentially statistical fitting and struggles to uncover the physical causal mechanisms of the atmosphere. Physics-oriented mechanism research still heavily relies on domain knowledge and cumbersome engineering operations of human scientists, becoming a bottleneck restricting the efficiency of Earth system science exploration. Here, we propose TianJi - the first "AI meteorologist" system capable of autonomously driving complex numerical models to verify physical mechanisms. Powered by a large language model-driven multi-agent architecture, TianJi can autonomously conduct literature research and generate scientific hypotheses. We further decouple scientific research into cognitive planning and engineering execution: the meta-planner interprets hypotheses and devises experimental roadmaps, while a cohort of specialized worker agents collaboratively complete data preparation, model configuration, and multi-dimensional result analysis. In two classic atmospheric dynamic scenarios (squall-line cold pools and typhoon track deflections), TianJi accomplishes expert-level end-to-end experimental operations with zero human intervention, compressing the research cycle to a few hours. It also delivers detailed result analyses and autonomously judges and explains the validity of the hypotheses from outputs. TianJi reveals that the role of AI in Earth system science is transitioning from a "black-box predictor" to an "interpretable scientific collaborator", offering a new paradigm for high-throughput exploration of scientific mechanisms.

zh.title: TianJi：用于发现大气科学物理机制的自主AI气象学家
zh.summary: TianJi是一个自主AI系统，采用多智能体LLM架构进行大气动力学科学研究，无需人类干预。该系统完成了关于冷池和台风偏转等现象的专家级研究，将研究周期从数周减少到数小时。

fr.title: TianJi : Un météorologue IA autonome pour découvrir les mécanismes physiques en sciences atmosphériques
fr.summary: TianJi est un système IA autonome utilisant une architecture multi-agents LLM pour mener des recherches scientifiques en dynamique atmosphérique sans intervention humaine. Le système a réalisé des recherches de niveau expert sur des phénomènes tels que les masses d'air froid et la déviation des typhons, réduisant les cycles de recherche de semaines à heures.

de.title: TianJi: Ein autonomer KI-Meteorologe zur Entdeckung physikalischer Mechanismen in der Atmosphärenwissenschaft
de.summary: TianJi ist ein autonomes KI-System, das eine Multi-Agent-LLM-Architektur nutzt, um wissenschaftliche Forschung in der Atmosphärendynamik ohne menschliches Eingreifen durchzuführen. Das System hat Expertenforschung zu Phänomenen wie Kaltluftpools und Taifunablenkung durchgeführt und die Forschungszyklen von Wochen auf Stunden reduziert.

es.title: TianJi: Un meteorólogo IA autónomo para descubrir mecanismos físicos en ciencias atmosféricas
es.summary: TianJi es un sistema IA autónomo que utiliza una arquitectura LLM multiagente para realizar investigaciones científicas en dinámica atmosférica sin intervención humana. El sistema ha completado investigaciones de nivel experto en fenómenos como piscinas de aire frío y deflexión de tifones, reduciendo los ciclos de investigación de semanas a horas.

title: SkyNet: Belief-Aware Planning for Partially-Observable Stochastic Games
summary: SkyNet extends MuZero for partially observable, stochastic multi-player games by adding auxiliary objectives that help models maintain uncertainty representation. In testing on the Skyjo card game, SkyNet achieved a 75.3% win rate against baseline, representing a +194 Elo improvement.
content:
arXiv:2603.27751v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In 2019, Google DeepMind released MuZero, a model-based reinforcement learning method that achieves strong results in perfect-information games by combining learned dynamics models with Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS). However, comparatively little work has extended MuZero to partially observable, stochastic, multi-player environments, where agents must act under uncertainty about hidden state. Such settings arise not only in card games but in domains such as autonomous negotiation, financial trading, and multi-agent robotics. In the absence of explicit belief modeling, MuZero's latent encoding has no dedicated mechanism for representing uncertainty over unobserved variables.
  To address this, we introduce SkyNet (Belief-Aware MuZero), which adds ego-conditioned auxiliary heads for winner prediction and rank estimation to the standard MuZero architecture. These objectives encourage the latent state to retain information predictive of outcomes under partial observability, without requiring explicit belief-state tracking or changes to the search algorithm.
  We evaluate SkyNet on Skyjo, a partially observable, non-zero-sum, stochastic card game, using a decision-granularity environment, transformer-based encoding, and a curriculum of heuristic opponents with self-play. In 1000-game head-to-head evaluations at matched checkpoints, SkyNet achieves a 75.3% peak win rate against the baseline (+194 Elo, $p < 10^{-50}$). SkyNet also outperforms the baseline against heuristic opponents (0.720 vs.\ 0.466 win rate). Critically, the belief-aware model initially underperforms the baseline but decisively surpasses it once training throughput is sufficient, suggesting that belief-aware auxiliary supervision improves learned representations under partial observability, but only given adequate data flow.

zh.title: SkyNet：部分可观测随机博弈的信念感知规划
zh.summary: SkyNet扩展了MuZero以用于部分可观测、随机多人游戏，通过添加辅助目标来帮助模型维护不确定性表示。在Skyjo纸牌游戏的测试中，SkyNet对基准线的胜率达到75.3%，代表了+194等级分的提升。

fr.title: SkyNet : Planification Consciente des Croyances pour les Jeux Stochastiques Partiellement Observables
fr.summary: SkyNet étend MuZero pour les jeux multi-joueurs partiellement observables et stochastiques en ajoutant des objectifs auxiliaires qui aident les modèles à maintenir la représentation de l'incertitude. Lors des tests sur le jeu de cartes Skyjo, SkyNet a atteint un taux de victoire de 75,3% contre le baseline, représentant une amélioration de +194 Elo.

de.title: SkyNet: Belief-aware Planung für partiell beobachtbare stochastische Spiele
de.summary: SkyNet erweitert MuZero für teilweise beobachtbare, stochastische Mehrspielerspiele durch Hinzufügen von Hilfsobjektiven, die Modellen helfen, die Unsicherheitsdarstellung zu bewahren. Bei Tests auf dem Kartenspiel Skyjo erreichte SkyNet eine Gewinnquote von 75,3% gegenüber dem Baseline, was einer +194-Elo-Verbesserung entspricht.

es.title: SkyNet: Planificación Consciente de Creencias para Juegos Estocásticos Parcialmente Observables
es.summary: SkyNet extiende MuZero para juegos multiusuario parcialmente observables y estocásticos al agregar objetivos auxiliares que ayuden a los modelos a mantener la representación de la incertidumbre. En las pruebas del juego de cartas Skyjo, SkyNet logró una tasa de victoria del 75,3% contra el baseline, representando una mejora de +194 Elo.

title: Let the Agent Steer: Closed-Loop Ranking Optimization via Influence Exchange
summary: Sortify is the first fully autonomous LLM-driven ranking optimization agent deployed in production recommendation systems, reframing optimization as an influence allocation problem. In two markets, the agent increased gross merchandise value by up to 9.2% and peak orders by 12.5%.
content:
arXiv:2603.27765v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recommendation ranking is fundamentally an influence allocation problem: a sorting formula distributes ranking influence among competing factors, and the business outcome depends on finding the optimal "exchange rates" among them. However, offline proxy metrics systematically misjudge how influence reallocation translates to online impact, with asymmetric bias across metrics that a single calibration factor cannot correct.
  We present Sortify, the first fully autonomous LLM-driven ranking optimization agent deployed in a large-scale production recommendation system. The agent reframes ranking optimization as continuous influence exchange, closing the full loop from diagnosis to parameter deployment without human intervention. It addresses structural problems through three mechanisms: (1) a dual-channel framework grounded in Savage's Subjective Expected Utility (SEU) that decouples offline-online transfer correction (Belief channel) from constraint penalty adjustment (Preference channel); (2) an LLM meta-controller operating on framework-level parameters rather than low-level search variables; (3) a persistent Memory DB with 7 relational tables for cross-round learning. Its core metric, Influence Share, provides a decomposable measure where all factor contributions sum to exactly 100%.
  Sortify has been deployed across two Southeast Asian markets. In Country A, the agent pushed GMV from -3.6% to +9.2% within 7 rounds with peak orders reaching +12.5%. In Country B, a cold-start deployment achieved +4.15% GMV/UU and +3.58% Ads Revenue in a 7-day A/B test, leading to full production rollout.

zh.title: 让智能体掌舵：通过影响力交换的闭环排名优化
zh.summary: Sortify是第一个完全自主的LLM驱动的排名优化智能体，部署在生产推荐系统中，将优化重新定义为影响力分配问题。在两个市场中，该智能体将商品总成交额增加了最多9.2%，峰值订单增加了12.5%。

fr.title: Laissez l'Agent Diriger : Optimisation du Classement en Boucle Fermée via l'Échange d'Influence
fr.summary: Sortify est le premier agent d'optimisation de classement entièrement autonome piloté par LLM déployé dans les systèmes de recommandation en production, redéfinissant l'optimisation comme un problème d'allocation d'influence. Sur deux marchés, l'agent a augmenté la valeur marchande brute jusqu'à 9,2% et les commandes maximales de 12,5%.

de.title: Lass den Agenten Lenken: Geschlossene Rangoptimierung durch Einflussaustausch
de.summary: Sortify ist der erste vollständig autonome LLM-gesteuerte Rangoptimierungsagent, der in Produktionsempfehlungssystemen eingesetzt wird und die Optimierung als Einflussallokationsproblem neu definiert. Auf zwei Märkten erhöhte der Agent den Bruttowert der Waren um bis zu 9,2% und die Spitzenbestellungen um 12,5%.

es.title: Deja que el Agente Dirija: Optimización de Clasificación en Lazo Cerrado mediante Intercambio de Influencia
es.summary: Sortify es el primer agente de optimización de clasificación completamente autónomo impulsado por LLM implementado en sistemas de recomendación en producción, redefiniendo la optimización como un problema de asignación de influencia. En dos mercados, el agente aumentó el valor bruto de mercancías hasta 9,2% e incrementó los pedidos pico en 12,5%.

title: CARGO: Carbon-Aware Gossip Orchestration in Smart Shipping
summary: CARGO is a federated learning framework for maritime networks that balances accuracy, carbon footprint, and reliability through serverless gossip protocols and adaptive participation. The system dynamically adjusts compression and communication based on real-time conditions in shipping operations.
content:
arXiv:2603.27857v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Smart shipping operations increasingly depend on collaborative AI, yet the underlying data are generated across vessels with uneven connectivity, limited backhaul, and clear commercial sensitivity. In such settings, server-coordinated FL remains a weak systems assumption, depending on a reachable aggregation point and repeated wide-area synchronization, both of which are difficult to guarantee in maritime networks. A serverless gossip approach therefore represents a more natural approach, but existing methods still treat communication mainly as an optimization bottleneck, rather than as a resource that must be managed jointly with carbon cost, reliability, and long-term participation balance. In this context, this paper presents CARGO, a carbon-aware gossip orchestration framework for smart-shipping. CARGO separates learning into a control and a data plane. The data plane performs local optimization with compressed gossip exchange, while the control plane decides, at each round, which vessels should participate, which communication edges should be activated, how aggressively updates should be compressed, and when recovery actions should be triggered. We evaluate CARGO under a predictive-maintenance scenario using operational bulk-carrier engine data and a trace-driven maritime communication protocol that captures client dropout, partial participation, packet loss, and multiple connectivity regimes, derived from mobility-aware vessel interactions. Across the tested stress settings, CARGO consistently remains in the high-accuracy regime while reducing carbon footprint and communication overheads, compared to accuracy-competitive decentralized baselines. Overall, the conducted performance evaluation demonstrates that CARGO is a feasible and practical solution for reliable and resource-conscious maritime AI deployment.

zh.title: CARGO：智能航运中的碳感知八卦编排
zh.summary: CARGO是一种海事网络的联邦学习框架，通过无服务器八卦协议和自适应参与来平衡准确性、碳足迹和可靠性。该系统根据航运操作中的实时条件动态调整压缩和通信。

fr.title: CARGO : Orchestration de Commérages Conscients du Carbone dans le Transport Maritime Intelligent
fr.summary: CARGO est un cadre d'apprentissage fédéré pour les réseaux maritimes qui équilibre la précision, l'empreinte carbone et la fiabilité grâce à des protocoles de commérages sans serveur et une participation adaptative. Le système ajuste dynamiquement la compression et la communication en fonction des conditions en temps réel dans les opérations de transport maritime.

de.title: CARGO: Kohlenstoffbewusste Klatsch-Orchestrierung in intelligenter Schifffahrt
de.summary: CARGO ist ein föderiertes Lernframework für maritime Netzwerke, das Genauigkeit, Kohlenstoff-Fußabdruck und Zuverlässigkeit durch serverlose Klatsch-Protokolle und adaptive Teilnahme ausgleicht. Das System passt Komprimierung und Kommunikation dynamisch basierend auf Echtzeit-Bedingungen im Schiffsbetrieb an.

es.title: CARGO: Orquestación de Chismes Consciente del Carbono en Transporte Marítimo Inteligente
es.summary: CARGO es un marco de aprendizaje federado para redes marítimas que equilibra precisión, huella de carbono y confiabilidad a través de protocolos de chismes sin servidor y participación adaptativa. El sistema ajusta dinámicamente la compresión y la comunicación basándose en condiciones en tiempo real en operaciones de transporte marítimo.

title: GAAMA: Graph Augmented Associative Memory for Agents
summary: GAAMA creates hierarchical knowledge graphs of agent memories using episode preservation, atomic facts, and reflections to maintain coherent personalized behavior across sessions. On multi-session conversation benchmarks, it outperforms traditional RAG and vector retrieval approaches by 3-31 percentage points.
content:
arXiv:2603.27910v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI agents that interact with users across multiple sessions require persistent long-term memory to maintain coherent, personalized behavior. Current approaches either rely on flat retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), which loses structural relationships between memories, or use memory compression and vector retrieval that cannot capture the associative structure of multi-session conversations. There are few graph based techniques proposed in the literature, however they still suffer from hub dominated retrieval and poor hierarchical reasoning over evolving memory. We propose GAAMA, a graph-augmented associative memory system that constructs a concept-mediated hierarchical knowledge graph through a three-step pipeline: (1)~verbatim episode preservation from raw conversations, (2)~LLM-based extraction of atomic facts and topic-level concept nodes, and (3)~synthesis of higher-order reflections. The resulting graph uses four node types (episode, fact, reflection, concept) connected by five structural edge types, with concept nodes providing cross-cutting traversal paths that complement semantic similarity. Retrieval combines cosine-similarity-based $k$-nearest neighbor search with edge-type-aware Personalized PageRank (PPR) through an additive scoring function. On the LoCoMo-10 benchmark (1,540 questions across 10 multi-session conversations), GAAMA achieves 78.9\% mean reward, outperforming a tuned RAG baseline (75.0\%), HippoRAG (69.9\%), A-Mem (47.2\%), and Nemori (52.1\%). Ablation analysis shows that augmenting graph-traversal-based ranking (Personalized PageRank) with semantic search consistently improves over pure semantic search on graph nodes (+1.0 percentage point overall).

zh.title: GAAMA：用于代理的图增强关联记忆
zh.summary: GAAMA使用事件保存、原子事实和反思来创建代理记忆的分层知识图，以维持会话间的连贯个性化行为。在多会话对话基准上，它的性能比传统的RAG和向量检索方法高3-31个百分点。

fr.title: GAAMA : Mémoire Associative Augmentée par Graphe pour les Agents
fr.summary: GAAMA crée des graphes de connaissances hiérarchiques des mémoires d'agents en utilisant la préservation d'épisodes, des faits atomiques et des réflexions pour maintenir un comportement personnalisé cohérent entre les sessions. Sur les repères de conversation multi-sessions, il surpasse les approches RAG traditionnelles et de récupération vectorielle de 3 à 31 points de pourcentage.

de.title: GAAMA: Graphenverstärkte assoziative Erinnerung für Agenten
de.summary: GAAMA erstellt hierarchische Wissensgraphen von Agentenerinnerungen mithilfe von Episode Preservation, atomaren Fakten und Reflexionen, um kohärentes personalisiertes Verhalten über Sessions hinweg zu bewahren. Bei Multi-Session-Gesprächs-Benchmarks übertrifft es traditionelle RAG- und Vektor-Abruf-Ansätze um 3-31 Prozentpunkte.

es.title: GAAMA: Memoria Asociativa Aumentada por Gráfico para Agentes
es.summary: GAAMA crea gráficos de conocimiento jerárquicos de memorias de agentes usando preservación de episodios, hechos atómicos y reflexiones para mantener un comportamiento personalizado coherente entre sesiones. En evaluaciones de conversación multi-sesión, supera a los enfoques tradicionales de RAG y recuperación vectorial en 3-31 puntos porcentuales.

title: GEAKG: Generative Executable Algorithm Knowledge Graphs
summary: GEAKG represents algorithmic knowledge as executable graphs where nodes are code operators and edges encode learned composition patterns, enabling zero-shot transfer across domains. Case studies demonstrate that algorithmic expertise learned in neural architecture search transfers directly to combinatorial optimization problems.
content:
arXiv:2603.27922v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In the context of algorithms for problem solving, procedural knowledge -- the know-how of algorithm design and operator composition -- remains implicit in code, lost between runs, and must be re-engineered for each new domain. Knowledge graphs (KGs) have proven effective for organizing declarative knowledge, yet current KG paradigms provide limited support for representing procedural knowledge as executable, learnable graph structures. We introduce \textit{Generative Executable Algorithm Knowledge Graphs} (GEAKG), a class of KGs whose nodes store executable operators, whose edges encode learned composition patterns, and whose traversal generates solutions. A GEAKG is \emph{generative} (topology and operators are synthesized by a Large Language Model), \emph{executable} (every node is runnable code), and \emph{transferable} (learned patterns generalize zero-shot across domains). The framework is domain-agnostic at the engine level: the same three-layer architecture and Ant Colony Optimization (ACO)-based learning engine can be instantiated across domains, parameterized by a pluggable ontology (\texttt{RoleSchema}). Two case studies -- sharing no domain-specific framework code -- provide concrete evidence for this framework hypothesis: (1)~Neural Architecture Search across 70 cross-dataset transfer pairs on two tabular benchmarks, and (2)~Combinatorial Optimization, where knowledge learned on the Traveling Salesman Problem transfers zero-shot to scheduling and assignment domains. Taken together, the results support that algorithmic expertise can be explicitly represented, learned, and transferred as executable knowledge graphs.

zh.title: GEAKG：生成可执行算法知识图
zh.summary: GEAKG将算法知识表示为可执行图，其中节点是代码操作符，边编码学习的组成模式，实现跨域零样本迁移。案例研究表明，神经架构搜索中学习的算法专业知识直接迁移到组合优化问题。

fr.title: GEAKG : Graphes de Connaissance d'Algorithmes Exécutables Générés
fr.summary: GEAKG représente la connaissance algorithmique comme des graphes exécutables où les nœuds sont des opérateurs de code et les arêtes encodent des motifs de composition appris, permettant un transfert zéro-shot entre les domaines. Les études de cas démontrent que l'expertise algorithmique apprise dans la recherche d'architecture neuronale se transfère directement aux problèmes d'optimisation combinatoire.

de.title: GEAKG: Generative ausführbare Algorithmus-Wissensgraphen
de.summary: GEAKG stellt algorithmisches Wissen als ausführbare Graphen dar, in denen Knoten Code-Operatoren sind und Kanten gelernte Kompositionsmuster kodieren und ermöglichen Zero-Shot-Transfer über Domänen hinweg. Fallstudien zeigen, dass algorithmische Expertise, die bei der Suche nach neuronalen Architekturen gelernt wurde, direkt auf kombinatorische Optimierungsprobleme übertragen wird.

es.title: GEAKG: Gráficos de Conocimiento de Algoritmo Ejecutable Generativo
es.summary: GEAKG representa el conocimiento algorítmico como gráficos ejecutables donde los nodos son operadores de código y los bordes codifican patrones de composición aprendidos, habilitando transferencia cero-shot entre dominios. Los estudios de caso demuestran que la experiencia algorítmica aprendida en la búsqueda de arquitectura neural se transfiere directamente a problemas de optimización combinatoria.

title: CARV: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Compositional Analogical Reasoning in Multimodal LLMs
summary: CARV is a benchmark revealing significant limitations in multimodal LLMs' compositional reasoning ability, where models must extract rules from multiple examples and apply them to new contexts. State-of-the-art models achieve only 40.4% accuracy on the task, far below human-level 100%.
content:
arXiv:2603.27958v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Analogical reasoning tests a fundamental aspect of human cognition: mapping the relation from one pair of objects to another. Existing evaluations of this ability in multimodal large language models (MLLMs) overlook the ability to compose rules from multiple sources, a critical component of higher-order intelligence. To close this gap, we introduce CARV (Compositional Analogical Reasoning in Vision), a novel task together with a 5,500-sample dataset as the first diagnostic benchmark. We extend the analogy from a single pair to multiple pairs, which requires MLLMs to extract symbolic rules from each pair and compose new transformations. Evaluation on the state-of-the-art MLLMs reveals a striking performance gap: even Gemini-2.5 Pro achieving only 40.4% accuracy, far below human-level performance of 100%. Diagnostic analysis shows two consistent failure modes: (1) decomposing visual changes into symbolic rules, and (2) maintaining robustness under diverse or complex settings, highlighting the limitations of current MLLMs on this task.

zh.title: CARV：多模态大语言模型组成类比推理诊断基准
zh.summary: CARV是一个基准测试，揭示了多模态大语言模型在组成推理能力上的重大局限性，其中模型必须从多个例子中提取规则并将其应用于新的语境。最先进的模型在该任务上仅达到40.4%的准确率，远低于人类水平的100%。

fr.title: CARV : Un benchmark de diagnostic pour le raisonnement analogique compositionnel dans les LLM multimodaux
fr.summary: CARV est un benchmark révélant des limitations significatives dans la capacité de raisonnement compositionnel des LLM multimodaux, où les modèles doivent extraire des règles à partir de plusieurs exemples et les appliquer à de nouveaux contextes. Les modèles de pointe n'atteignent que 40,4% de précision sur cette tâche, bien en deçà du niveau humain de 100%.

de.title: CARV: Ein diagnostischer Benchmark für kompositorisches analoges Denken in multimodalen LLMs
de.summary: CARV ist ein Benchmark, der erhebliche Einschränkungen in der kompositorischen Denkfähigkeit multimodaler LLMs offenbart, bei dem Modelle Regeln aus mehreren Beispielen extrahieren und auf neue Kontexte anwenden müssen. State-of-the-Art-Modelle erreichen bei dieser Aufgabe nur 40,4% Genauigkeit, weit unter dem menschlichen Niveau von 100%.

es.title: CARV: Un benchmark de diagnóstico para el razonamiento analógico composicional en LLM multimodales
es.summary: CARV es un benchmark que revela limitaciones significativas en la capacidad de razonamiento composicional de los LLM multimodales, donde los modelos deben extraer reglas de múltiples ejemplos y aplicarlas a nuevos contextos. Los modelos de última generación logran solo 40.4% de precisión en la tarea, muy por debajo del nivel humano del 100%.

title: SARL: Label-Free Reinforcement Learning by Rewarding Reasoning Topology
summary: SARL is a reinforcement learning framework that improves reasoning models by rewarding the structure and topology of thinking processes rather than final answers, enabling learning in domains where correctness cannot be verified. The approach achieves 9-34% gains across math and open-ended reasoning tasks without labeled supervision.
content:
arXiv:2603.27977v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Reinforcement learning has become central to improving large reasoning models, but its success still relies heavily on verifiable rewards or labeled supervision. This limits its applicability to open ended domains where correctness is ambiguous and cannot be verified. Moreover, reasoning trajectories remain largely unconstrained, and optimization towards final answer can favor early exploitation over generalization. In this work, we ask whether general reasoning ability can be improved by teaching models how to think (the structure of reasoning) rather than what to produce (the outcome of reasoning) and extend traditional RLVR to open ended settings. We introduce structure aware reinforcement learning (SARL), a label free framework that constructs a per response Reasoning Map from intermediate thinking steps and rewards its small world topology, inspired by complex networks and the functional organization of the human brain. SARL encourages reasoning trajectories that are both locally coherent and globally efficient, shifting supervision from destination to path. Our experiments on Qwen3-4B show SARL surpasses ground truth based RL and prior label free RL baselines, achieving the best average gain of 9.1% under PPO and 11.6% under GRPO on math tasks and 34.6% under PPO and 30.4% under GRPO on open ended tasks. Beyond good performance, SARL also exhibits lower KL divergence, higher policy entropy, indicating a more stable and exploratory training and generalized reasoning ability.

zh.title: SARL：通过奖励推理拓扑的无标签强化学习
zh.summary: SARL是一个强化学习框架，通过奖励思考过程的结构和拓扑而非最终答案来改进推理模型，使得在无法验证正确性的领域中进行学习成为可能。该方法在数学和开放式推理任务中实现了9-34%的提升，无需标签监督。

fr.title: SARL : Apprentissage par renforcement sans étiquette en récompensant la topologie du raisonnement
fr.summary: SARL est un framework d'apprentissage par renforcement qui améliore les modèles de raisonnement en récompensant la structure et la topologie des processus de réflexion plutôt que les réponses finales, permettant l'apprentissage dans les domaines où la correction ne peut pas être vérifiée. L'approche réalise des gains de 9-34% sur les tâches de mathématiques et de raisonnement ouvert sans supervision étiquetée.

de.title: SARL: Verstärkungslernen ohne Beschriftung durch Belohnung der Reasoning-Topologie
de.summary: SARL ist ein Rahmenwerk für verstärkendes Lernen, das Argumentationsmodelle verbessert, indem es die Struktur und Topologie von Denkprozessen anstelle von endgültigen Antworten belohnt, was Lernen in Bereichen ermöglicht, in denen Korrektheit nicht überprüft werden kann. Der Ansatz erreicht Gewinne von 9-34% bei Mathematik- und offenen Argumentationsaufgaben ohne gekennzeichnete Überwachung.

es.title: SARL: Aprendizaje por refuerzo sin etiquetas recompensando la topología del razonamiento
es.summary: SARL es un marco de aprendizaje por refuerzo que mejora los modelos de razonamiento recompensando la estructura y topología de los procesos de pensamiento en lugar de respuestas finales, lo que permite el aprendizaje en dominios donde no se puede verificar la corrección. El enfoque logra ganancias de 9-34% en tareas de matemáticas y razonamiento abierto sin supervisión etiquetada.

title: HeteroHub: An Applicable Data Management Framework for Heterogeneous Multi-Embodied Agent System
summary: HeteroHub is a data management framework that unifies static metadata, multimodal training datasets, and real-time sensor streams to coordinate multiple embodied AI agents with diverse capabilities. The framework enables task-aware training and closed-loop control for complex multi-agent coordination.
content:
arXiv:2603.28010v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Heterogeneous Multi-Embodied Agent Systems involve coordinating multiple embodied agents with diverse capabilities to accomplish tasks in dynamic environments. This process requires the collection, generation, and consumption of massive, heterogeneous data, which primarily falls into three categories: static knowledge regarding the agents, tasks, and environments; multimodal training datasets tailored for various AI models; and high-frequency sensor streams. However, existing frameworks lack a unified data management infrastructure to support the real-world deployment of such systems. To address this gap, we present \textbf{HeteroHub}, a data-centric framework that integrates static metadata, task-aligned training corpora, and real-time data streams. The framework supports task-aware model training, context-sensitive execution, and closed-loop control driven by real-world feedback. In our demonstration, HeteroHub successfully coordinates multiple embodied AI agents to execute complex tasks, illustrating how a robust data management framework can enable scalable, maintainable, and evolvable embodied AI systems.

zh.title: HeteroHub：异构多具身代理系统的适用数据管理框架
zh.summary: HeteroHub是一个数据管理框架，统一了静态元数据、多模态训练数据集和实时传感器流，以协调具有不同能力的多个具身AI代理。该框架为复杂的多代理协调启用了任务感知训练和闭环控制。

fr.title: HeteroHub : Un framework de gestion de données applicable pour un système d'agents multi-incarnés hétérogènes
fr.summary: HeteroHub est un framework de gestion de données qui unifie les métadonnées statiques, les ensembles de données d'entraînement multimodales et les flux de capteurs en temps réel pour coordonner plusieurs agents IA incarnés aux capacités diverses. Le framework permet l'entraînement conscient des tâches et le contrôle en boucle fermée pour la coordination multi-agents complexe.

de.title: HeteroHub: Ein anwendbares Datenverwaltungs-Framework für heterogene Multi-Embodied-Agent-Systeme
de.summary: HeteroHub ist ein Datenverwaltungs-Framework, das statische Metadaten, multimodale Trainingsdatensätze und Echtzeit-Sensorströme vereinheitlicht, um mehrere verkörperte KI-Agenten mit unterschiedlichen Fähigkeiten zu koordinieren. Das Framework ermöglicht aufgabenbewusste Schulung und Regelkreissteuerung für komplexe Multi-Agent-Koordination.

es.title: HeteroHub: Un marco de gestión de datos aplicable para un sistema heterogéneo de agentes multiencarnados
es.summary: HeteroHub es un marco de gestión de datos que unifica metadatos estáticos, conjuntos de datos de entrenamiento multimodales y flujos de sensores en tiempo real para coordinar múltiples agentes de IA encarnados con capacidades diversas. El marco permite entrenamiento consciente de tareas y control de bucle cerrado para la coordinación compleja de múltiples agentes.
